channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Easter Observance Amid Ongoing Violence in Nigeria
item.description: Easter na di celebration wey mark the resurrection of Jesus Christ, and e still dey serve as moral compass for society. E no just be religion matter, e dey remind us say life go always conquer death, ...

item.title: Gulf States Request UN Authorization to Safeguard Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Several Gulf countries have formally requested the United Nations to authorize a multinational force tasked with securing the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime passage for global oil shipments. Th...

item.title: Casualty Figures from the US-Israel Conflict with Iran as of 2 April
item.description: Since 28 February, the United States and Israel have launched military strikes against Iran, triggering retaliatory attacks from Iran on Israeli and American positions, as well as on Gulf states. The ...

item.title: High Court Confirms Legality of Murder Charges After Disclosure Review
item.description: The Barbados High Court has dismissed a constitutional challenge filed by two men, Kemar Mario Greene and Romono Anthony Drayton Greene, who were charged with the murder of Simeon Legall and spent ove...

item.title: The Rich Heritage of Surpur Paintings in North Karnataka
item.description: Surpur paintings, originating from the Kalyana Karnataka region in North Karnataka, are an artistic tradition with a history of around 400 years. This style flourished under the Bedar Nayaks, rulers o...

item.title: Opposition urges fuel price transparency as retailers warned to implement excise cuts promptly
item.description: The Australian consumer watchdog, the ACCC, has urged fuel retailers to pass on recent excise cuts without delay, as opposition leader Angus Taylor called for greater transparency during the ongoing f...

item.title: Kenya Orders Recall of Peanut Butter Due to Dangerous Aflatoxin Contamination
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has issued an urgent recall of several peanut butter brands after laboratory tests found dangerously high levels of aflatoxin, a toxic substance linked to liver damage ...

item.title: AJK High Court Orders Legal Process Before Detaining or Deporting Afghan Nationals
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) High Court has taken a strong stance protecting Afghan nationals residing in the region, ruling that no individual can be detained or deported without proper legal pro...

item.title: Elderly residents in Verulam and Chatsworth face violent home invasions
item.description: Elderly residents in the Verulam and Chatsworth areas are increasingly being targeted by violent criminals, raising serious concerns about community safety. On Monday morning, a 68-year-old woman in D...

item.title: Tanzania and Switzerland Discuss Strengthening Bilateral Cooperation
item.description: The Tanzanian Ambassador to Mozambique, Hamad Hamad, recently met with the Swiss Ambassador, Nicolas Randin, at the Tanzanian Embassy in Maputo. The meeting was aimed at deepening the diplomatic relat...

item.title: Ebonyi Governor Condemns Murder of Isinkwo Community Monarch, Vows Justice
item.description: Di Governor of Ebonyi State, Francis Nwifuru, don strongly condemn di killing of Eze Josephat Ikegwu, di traditional ruler of Isinkwo Community for Onicha Local Government Area. Di Governor describe d...

item.title: Former Turnstile guitarist faces attempted murder charges over attack on bandmate’s father
item.description: Brady Ebert, 33, a former guitarist and founding member of the US hardcore punk band Turnstile, has been charged with attempted murder after allegedly running over the father of Turnstile's lead singe...

item.title: A comprehensive guide to notable Easter bunny appearances in film
item.description: This article explores the most memorable appearances of rabbits in cinema, from comical and whimsical portrayals to sinister and unsettling ones. It highlights twenty films, ranging from classics like...

item.title: Deputy President Kindiki Appeals for Patience as Modern Gikomba Market Construction Begins
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has called on the more than 6,300 traders at New Gikomba Market to remain patient as the government prepares to start construction of a modern seven-storey market on 2...

item.title: FG Declares Good Friday and Easter Monday as Public Holidays for 2026
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don officially announce say Friday, April 3 and Monday, April 6, 2026 go be public holidays to mark Easter celebration. The announcement come from the Minister of Int...

item.title: Philippines assigns local names to Kalayaan Island Group features amid South China Sea dispute
item.description: MANILA — China has voiced strong objections to the Philippines' recent move to assign standardized Filipino names to over 100 geographical features in the Kalayaan Island Group (KIG), located in the W...

item.title: Understanding That Parenting is About Growth, Not Perfection
item.description: Parenting na si kitu cha kuwa perfect. Article hii inaeleza kwa undani kwamba wazazi wanapaswa kukubali kuwa kutakuwa na mistakes, na hizo mistakes ni sehemu ya kujifunza na kuimarisha uhusiano na wat...

item.title: AAP appoints Ashok Mittal as new Rajya Sabha deputy leader, replaces Raghav Chadha
item.description: On Thursday, the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) announced the replacement of Raghav Chadha with Ashok Mittal as the party's deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha. Party sources explained that a formal letter was se...

item.title: Jonathan the Giant Tortoise Confirmed Alive Following Fake Death Report
item.description: Jonathan, a Seychelles giant tortoise believed to be the world's oldest living land animal at around 193 years old, has been confirmed alive after a false death report circulated online. The misleadin...

item.title: Emergency services search Brisbane River for missing woman near South Brisbane CityCat terminal
item.description: Emergency services in Brisbane are conducting an urgent search following reports that a woman has gone missing in the Brisbane River near the South Brisbane CityCat terminal on Good Friday. Queensland...
